name: pfSense Firewall
description: pfSense REST API for managing firewall rules, aliases, NAT, and system configuration on pfSense CE and pfSense Plus

auth_config.note: Supports Basic auth, JWT bearer tokens, and API key authentication

actions.name: List Firewall Rules
actions.description: Retrieve all firewall rules configured on pfSense

actions.name: Create Firewall Rule
actions.description: Create a new firewall rule to allow or block traffic

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Rule type: pass, block, or reject

parameters.name: interface
parameters.description: Interface to apply the rule (e.g., wan, lan)

parameters.name: ipprotocol
parameters.description: IP protocol: inet (IPv4), inet6 (IPv6), or inet46 (both)

parameters.name: protocol
parameters.description: Protocol: tcp, udp, icmp, or any

parameters.name: src
parameters.description: Source address (IP, CIDR, alias name, or any)

parameters.name: dst
parameters.description: Destination address (IP, CIDR, alias name, or any)

parameters.name: top
parameters.description: Place rule at the top of the ruleset

actions.name: Delete Firewall Rule
actions.description: Delete an existing firewall rule by tracker ID

parameters.name: tracker
parameters.description: Tracker ID of the firewall rule to delete

actions.name: List Aliases
actions.description: Retrieve all firewall aliases (address groups for IPs, ports, URLs)

actions.name: Create Alias
actions.description: Create a new firewall alias for grouping IPs, ports, or URLs

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Alias type: host, network, port, or url

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: List of addresses, networks, or ports to include

actions.name: Delete Alias
actions.description: Delete a firewall alias by name

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Name/ID of the alias to delete

actions.name: Get System Status
actions.description: Retrieve pfSense system status including version, uptime, and resource usage

actions.name: List Interfaces
actions.description: Retrieve all network interfaces and their configuration

actions.name: Apply Firewall Changes
actions.description: Apply pending firewall rule changes to make them active
